behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the core failure is parsing/segmentation logic on one command string; it can fail inside a single parser/classifier function boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Unit candidate setup = pass a multi-line shell string with assignments + command to segmenter; assertion = returns separate segments; catches failure = whole block returned as one token. Adjacent Integration candidate setup = run full hook pipeline with same input; assertion = internal segments count >1; catches same failure but adds wiring noise without increasing detection power. Rejection-gate: higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing only on segmentation semantics, not OS/network/subprocess/model output.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the segmentation function with newline-separated shell input and assert segment boundaries are emitted per statement (including assignment lines and final command line).

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: incident boundary is segmentation -> trigger classification handoff; unit-testing only the matcher or only the segmenter can miss this interaction. REAL-INFRA: no real subprocess/HTTP required because behavior text is about recognizing a command block string. LLM/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Integration candidate setup = feed realistic PostToolUse payload containing multi-line block into hook handler with temp state sink; assertion = classification is PR-create trigger; catches failure = handler marks `not_a_trigger` due to monolithic parsing. Adjacent Unit candidate setup = call trigger-matcher with already-cleaned `gh pr create`; assertion = trigger true; failure caught = simple regex bug, but misses segmentation handoff bug. Rejection-gate: System disqualified by text describing recognition logic, not real `gh` execution.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test the PostToolUse parsing+classification path end-to-end in-process using a multi-line command payload (`VAR=...` lines then `gh pr create ...`) and assert trigger classification is PR-create.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: negative classification depends on the same segmentation + command-intent classifier interaction as behavior 2. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: none required. Candidate tests: Integration candidate setup = PostToolUse payload with assignments + `gh issue create`; assertion = classified as non-PR trigger; catches failure = broad matcher wrongly firing on any `gh ... create`. Adjacent Unit candidate setup = test regex against string `gh issue create`; assertion = no match; catches local pattern bug but can miss context assembly bug from multi-line segmentation. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text is purely command-type discrimination in hook logic.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test the same in-process PostToolUse pipeline with multi-line payload ending in `gh issue create` and assert it remains `not_a_trigger` (or equivalent non-PR classification).

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is classifier output handoff into state-building and persistence modules. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem persistence is part of behavior, but a tempdir/file write in-process is sufficient; no OS-specific primitives or subprocess semantics are required. LLM/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate tests: Integration candidate setup = inject detected PR-create event plus representative command/output metadata into hook and real temp filesystem path; assertion = state file contains reconstructed PR identifier fields expected by review loop; catches failure = trigger true but identifier missing/wrong or not persisted. Adjacent Unit candidate setup = test identifier parser alone from a fixed string; assertion = parsed id; catches parser bugs but misses persistence contract bug. Rejection-gate: System disqualified because text says 'persist PR identifier' without requiring real CLI/network side effects.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test detection->identifier reconstruction->state persistence with a real temp file path; assert persisted state includes a valid PR identifier payload consumed by the review loop.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an inter-module contract between PostToolUse writer and PreToolUse reader/enforcer; testing either side alone can miss schema/key mismatches. REAL-INFRA: local file I/O suffices; no real subprocess/network needed. LLM/MULTI-STEP: no model decisions. Candidate tests: Integration candidate setup = run PostToolUse write path, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement against that state file; assertion = enforcement decision reflects gate-on behavior; catches failure = state exists but unreadable/incompatible schema. Adjacent System candidate setup = run hooks via actual shell subprocesses; assertion = same enforcement; catches environment issues but is not minimum for stated contract failure. Rejection-gate: higher System level is disqualified by behavior text emphasizing readable format/contract, not OS or subprocess edge behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration contract test across hooks: execute PostToolUse PR-trigger handling to write state, then execute PreToolUse enforcement read path and assert gate enforcement activates from that written state.
